title: Chesapeake Bay Seasoning
intro: This style of seasoning grew out of early 20th-century coastal seafood houses, where bold spice blends were built to season large batches of crab and shrimp. This version leans New England, with a lighter hand on salt and a cleaner, more herbal profile that lets the shellfish stay front and center.

stepGroups.title: Instructions
items: Grind the celery seed to a fine powder. This is critical. Whole or coarse celery seed won’t distribute evenly and you’ll lose that signature flavor.
Combine the ground celery seed and fine sea salt in a bowl. Mix thoroughly so the celery flavor is evenly carried by the salt.
Add the sweet and smoked paprika. Whisk until the color is uniform.
Stir in the bay leaf, thyme, dill, mustard, and peppers. Mix well.
Add the mace, nutmeg, ginger, red pepper flakes, and cayenne. Whisk until fully blended.
Pulse briefly in a spice grinder if needed for a tighter blend.
Let sit at least 12 hours before using.
